description: Improve harness-llm-wiki verification so batch runs distinguish runnable pipelines from blocked quality outcomes, enforce Obsidian-safe exports, and separate isolated verification from shared-vault validation.

userStories.title: Add structured paper verdict tiers to quick verify results
userStories.description: As a verification user, I want each quick-verify paper result to expose structured verdict tiers so that a completed pipeline run is not automatically treated as a usable wiki delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: Each per-paper result from `scripts/quick_verify_papers.py` includes a final verdict field with values drawn from `pipeline_runnable`, `quality_blocked`, or `usable_wiki_ready`
A `run_status=completed` paper is no longer labeled final success unless it also satisfies the usable-wiki rules
The structured verdict is serialized into the batch `summary.json` output for every processed file
python -m mypy docos/ exits with code 0
python -m pytest tests/ -q exits with code 0
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Classify gate-blocked runs as quality-blocked outcomes
userStories.description: As a verification user, I want gate-blocked runs to be classified as quality-blocked so that blocked papers do not inflate the final success count.
acceptanceCriteria: A paper with `gate.passed=false` is classified as `quality_blocked` instead of `usable_wiki_ready` in quick-verify results
The batch success counters exclude gate-blocked papers from the final usable-wiki tally
The per-paper payload still preserves `gate.decision` and gate reasons for blocked runs

python -m pytest tests/ -q exits with code 0

userStories.title: Classify pending-review runs as non-deliverable results

acceptanceCriteria: A paper with `review_status=pending` is not classified as `usable_wiki_ready` in quick-verify results
Pending-review papers are counted separately in the batch summary instead of being merged into final success totals
Per-paper summary data shows both the final verdict and the original `review_status` value together

python -m pytest tests/ -q exits with code 0

acceptanceCriteria: A processed paper with `entities=0`, `claims=0`, and `relations=0` is marked with a `knowledge_sparse` signal in its result payload
The batch summary includes a counter for papers marked knowledge-sparse
Knowledge-sparse classification does not require the run to fail outright; it remains observable alongside the main verdict
